legal.summary: Information acquired under the Act must be kept confidential; unauthorized disclosure is a criminal offence.

requirements.requirement: Ensure all persons performing functions under the Act maintain strict confidentiality of information acquired in the course of those functions
requirements.rationale: Breach of secrecy carries RM500,000/10 years — one of the highest penalties under the Act

requirements.requirement: Only disclose Act-related information for permitted purposes: purposes of the Act, civil/criminal proceedings, or with written CE consent
requirements.rationale: Three narrow exceptions define the boundaries of permitted disclosure

technical.summary: Technical controls for protecting Act-related information.

requirements.requirement: Implement information classification and handling procedures for Act-related data (incident reports, risk assessments, audit results, threat intelligence), with access restricted to authorized personnel
requirements.rationale: Technical controls must enforce the secrecy obligation across all systems and channels

requirements.requirement: Include Act secrecy obligations in staff training, employment contracts, and NDA arrangements for all personnel with access to Act-related information
requirements.rationale: Staff awareness and contractual commitments are the first line of defence against unauthorized disclosure

legal.summary: Directors and officers face personal criminal liability for corporate offences unless they prove due diligence. Corporate fines may be multiplied by 10x.

requirements.requirement: Directors, CEO, COO, managers and officers must be able to demonstrate that they exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of any offence by the body corporate
requirements.rationale: Reverse burden of proof — officers are deemed to have committed the offence unless they can prove due diligence and lack of knowledge/consent/connivance

requirements.requirement: Maintain evidence of board-level engagement with NCII compliance, including meeting minutes, compliance reports reviewed, decisions made, and resources allocated
requirements.rationale: Due diligence defence requires demonstrable evidence of active governance, not passive awareness

technical.summary: Technical controls enabling board-level visibility and due diligence evidence for corporate officer liability.

requirements.requirement: Implement board-level cybersecurity dashboard with real-time compliance metrics
requirements.rationale: Directors need continuous visibility into NCII compliance posture to demonstrate active oversight and due diligence

requirements.requirement: Deploy automated compliance monitoring for all NCII systems with alerting
requirements.rationale: Automated monitoring provides evidence that the body corporate maintained continuous compliance oversight, supporting due diligence defence

requirements.requirement: Maintain audit trail of all corporate officer access to NCII management systems
requirements.rationale: Demonstrates that officers actively engaged with compliance systems, supporting their due diligence defence under s52

governance.summary: Board governance framework to support due diligence defence.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board Cyber Security Committee or assign NCII compliance oversight to an existing board committee with regular reporting cadence
requirements.rationale: Structured board oversight is the foundation of a credible due diligence defence under s52

requirements.requirement: Ensure adequate resources (budget, staffing, tools) are allocated for NCII compliance, with board-approved budget for cyber security risk treatment
requirements.rationale: Demonstrating resource allocation is key evidence of due diligence by directors

legal.summary: Persons aggrieved by Chief Executive decisions may appeal to the Minister within 30 days; the Minister's decision is final.

requirements.requirement: File any appeal to the Minister within 30 days of being notified of the Chief Executive's decision
requirements.rationale: The 30-day appeal window is a strict deadline; failure to appeal within the period extinguishes the right of appeal

requirements.requirement: Accept that the Minister's decision on appeal is final and not subject to further administrative appeal
requirements.rationale: Understanding finality provisions enables proper legal strategy, including consideration of judicial review as an alternative remedy

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

requirements.requirement: Establish an appeal management procedure including deadline tracking, legal counsel engagement, evidence compilation, and board notification for material appeals
requirements.rationale: Structured appeal management prevents deadline misses and ensures the strongest case is presented

legal.summary: Informer identity and information are protected; no witness may be compelled to disclose informer identity except where the court determines it is necessary for justice.

requirements.requirement: Protect the identity of any person who reports cyber security offences or provides information to NACSA, in alignment with s50 informer protections
requirements.rationale: Informer protection encourages reporting and is a statutory obligation; breaching protection may deter future reporting

requirements.requirement: Implement anonymous or confidential reporting channels with technical controls that protect informer identity, including encrypted communication and restricted access to report details
requirements.rationale: Technical identity protection makes the statutory protection effective in practice

governance.summary: Governance for whistleblower and informer protection.

requirements.requirement: Establish a whistleblower protection policy referencing Act 854 s50 provisions, with anti-retaliation measures and regular staff awareness activities
requirements.rationale: Formal policy and awareness promote a culture of reporting while protecting informers

legal.summary: Agent provocateur evidence is admissible and the agent is not presumed unworthy of credit merely for involvement in the offence.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the legal team understands that s51 modifies common law rules on agent provocateur credibility, making such evidence admissible without the usual credibility presumption
requirements.rationale: Defence strategy must account for the enhanced admissibility of agent provocateur evidence under Act 854

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

requirements.requirement: Include Act 854 evidence provisions (s41, s42, s51) in the legal team's knowledge base and continuing education programme
requirements.rationale: Awareness of non-standard evidence rules prevents tactical legal errors in enforcement proceedings

legal.summary: No prosecution under the Act may be instituted without the written consent of the Public Prosecutor.

requirements.requirement: Understand that prosecution under Act 854 requires the Public Prosecutor's written consent, which may affect pre-trial negotiation strategy
requirements.rationale: The Public Prosecutor's consent requirement provides a checkpoint that may be relevant to defence strategy and compounding negotiations

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

requirements.requirement: Establish a prosecution response protocol including immediate legal counsel engagement, evidence preservation, board notification, and communication strategy
requirements.rationale: Structured prosecution response prevents evidence spoliation and ensures coordinated defence

legal.summary: The Chief Executive may compound prescribed offences with Public Prosecutor consent; compound amount shall not exceed 50% of the maximum fine.

requirements.requirement: Understand compounding options and that compound amounts cannot exceed 50% of the maximum fine for the offence
requirements.rationale: Compounding offers an alternative to prosecution that may be more cost-effective and less disruptive than trial

requirements.requirement: Accept that payment of compound releases the entity from further proceedings for the compounded offence
requirements.rationale: Understanding the finality of compounding helps in decision-making between compounding and contesting prosecution

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

requirements.requirement: Establish a decision framework for evaluating compounding offers versus contesting prosecution, with board approval for material amounts
requirements.rationale: Compounding decisions involve significant financial and reputational implications requiring governance oversight

s55.title: Protection against suits and legal proceedings

legal.summary: No action or suit may be brought against the CE, authorized officers, or persons acting in good faith under the Act.

requirements.requirement: Understand that the Chief Executive, authorized officers, and persons acting under the Act in good faith have statutory immunity from suits
requirements.rationale: Statutory immunity limits the entity's legal remedies against regulatory actions; alternative remedies such as s48 appeal must be pursued

requirements.requirement: Document compliance with directives and orders in good faith to benefit from the protection under s55(b) for persons acting in compliance
requirements.rationale: Documented good faith compliance may provide protection if the entity faces suit for actions taken in compliance with regulatory directives

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for managing statutory immunity implications.

requirements.requirement: Maintain an audit trail of compliance decisions and good faith efforts, documenting rationale, legal advice obtained, and actions taken under regulatory direction
requirements.rationale: Audit trail of good faith compliance supports both the entity's protection under s55(b) and its position in any subsequent proceedings
